steps.title: Identify the experiential advantage
steps.content: The passage explains the surprising result through this key phrase: “outweighed by participants' views of the experiential relevance of the two types of messengers.” The passage is saying that volunteers' direct experience with clinical trials outweighed (was more important than) the doctors' credentialed status in participants' minds.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
B explains participants' preference for volunteers by emphasizing that their direct experience with clinical trials outweighed doctors' credentialed status—precisely the experiential relevance the passage describes.
  • A misinterprets the finding as skepticism of experts
  • C confuses participants' evaluation ability with messengers' relevance
  • D shifts focus to message tone
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
